---
title: "Preface"
---

<div style="text-align: center; margin: 2em 0;">
![](sections/images/notes-to-icd10-logo.png){width=60% fig-alt="Notes to ICD-10 logo showing a clinical note feeding into a neural network producing code E11.65"}
</div>


# How to Read This Document {.unnumbered}

This document serves as both a technical report and a complete project record
for the Notes-to-ICD-10 system — a machine learning pipeline that automatically
predicts ICD-10 diagnostic codes from free-text clinical notes.

It is written for a wide audience. Use the guide below to find the sections
most relevant to you.

---

## Reader Guide {.unnumbered}

::: {.callout-note icon=false}
## 📋 For Business and Clinical Stakeholders
Read: **Executive Summary** → **Introduction** → **Results** → **Limitations**

These sections explain what the system does, why it matters, what it achieves,
and what its current boundaries are — without requiring a background in machine
learning or software engineering.
:::

::: {.callout-note icon=false}
## 🔬 For Data Scientists and ML Practitioners
Read: **Methodology** → **Experiments** → **Results** → **Conclusions**

These sections cover the model architecture, training strategy, ablation
experiments, calibration analysis, and key research findings in full technical
depth.
:::

::: {.callout-note icon=false}
## ⚙️ For Software Engineers and Developers
Read: **Dataset** → **Methodology** → **Inference Pipeline** → **Limitations**

These sections document the data pipeline, preprocessing decisions, inference
architecture, and known technical constraints relevant to production deployment.
:::

---

## Document Conventions {.unnumbered}

Throughout this document:

- **Callout boxes** like the ones above highlight key findings, warnings, or
  important constraints that readers should not skip.
- **Code listings** show exact commands or implementation details — safely
  skippable if you are not working with the codebase directly.
- Each major section opens with a **plain-language summary** that communicates
  the essential point before diving into technical detail.
- Tables marked with a 🏆 symbol contain the headline results for the section.

---

## Reproducibility {.unnumbered}

All experiments reported in this document are fully reproducible from the
project repository. The experiment registry (`outputs/experiments.json`) and
step-by-step reproduction guide (`Run notes.md`) are included in the
repository alongside the source code.

```
git clone https://github.com/Sidney-Bishop/notes-to-icd10
uv sync
uv run python verify_scripts.py
```
